summary: Import-time execution of AES-encrypted opaque payload with shipped key
details: On require(), index.js immediately calls pkg.run(), which decrypts a ~10KB hardcoded AES-GCM ciphertext (DEFAULT_FINAL_ENCODED_TEXT in src/config/defaults.js) using a passphrase (default-dev-passphrase) and salt (encode-npm-c-salt) shipped in the same package, then invokes the cleartext via `new Function("require", runnable)(require)` in src/pipeline/custom-codec-pipeline.js:53. Encrypting an executable blob with a key bundled alongside it has no security purpose — its sole effect is to hide the payload from static review. Because the decrypted code receives `require`, it can perform arbitrary actions (network I/O, filesystem access, credential reads, child process spawning) on the installer's machine the moment any consumer imports the package. The opacity of the payload combined with the canonical decode-and-eval shape and import-time auto-execution makes this an active supply-chain attack regardless of the specific decrypted contents.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
